meaning: n.小提琴,无意识而不停地拨弄vt.虚度时光,拉小提琴vi.拉小提琴,瞎搞
examples: He took the purse, put up his fiddle, and traveled on very pleased with his bargain.
The countryman stopped his fiddle, and left the miser to take his place at the gallows.
New York Gov. Eliot Spitzer and his staff have long played the press like a fiddle.
Flailing owners often fiddle with the menu or the concept, but it rarely works.
